title: English Grammar
subject: English
description: Nouns, verbs, adjectives, pronouns, and sentence structure

sections.name: Nouns (Naming Words)
sections.instruction: Nouns are words that name people, places, things, or animals.

questions.prompt: Select ALL the NOUNS in this sentence: 'The boy carries a bag to school.'
questions.hint: Nouns are names of people, places, or things.

questions.prompt: Which word is a PERSON?
questions.hint: A person is someone you can talk to!

questions.prompt: Which word is a PLACE?
questions.hint: A place is somewhere you can go.

questions.prompt: Select ALL the NOUNS: 'The cat sat on the mat.'
questions.hint: Look for the naming words - what things are mentioned?

sections.name: Verbs (Doing Words)
sections.instruction: Verbs are action words - they tell us what someone or something does.

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'The dog runs fast.'
questions.hint: A verb is an action word - what is the dog DOING?

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'She drinks water.'
questions.hint: What action is she doing?

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'We play in the yard.'
questions.hint: What are 'we' doing in the yard?

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'The bird sings a song.'
questions.hint: What does the bird do?

sections.name: Adjectives (Describing Words)
sections.instruction: Adjectives describe nouns - they tell us more about people, places, or things.

questions.prompt: What is the DESCRIBING word in: 'The red ball is on the table.'
questions.hint: Which word describes what the ball looks like?

questions.prompt: What is the DESCRIBING word in: 'Janelle has a new bag.'
questions.hint: Which word describes the bag?

questions.prompt: What is the ADJECTIVE in: 'The tall tree has green leaves.'
questions.hint: Which word tells us about the tree's size?

questions.prompt: Select ALL the adjectives: 'The big, brown dog has a long tail.'
questions.hint: Adjectives describe nouns. Which words describe the dog and tail?

sections.name: Pronouns
sections.instruction: Pronouns replace nouns so we don't repeat the same word.

questions.prompt: The BOYS are playing. Which pronoun replaces 'The boys'?
questions.hint: 'The boys' is more than one person!

questions.prompt: MY MOTHER AND I are baking. Which pronoun replaces 'My mother and I'?
questions.hint: When you include yourself, use 'we'!

questions.prompt: THE GIRL is reading. Which pronoun replaces 'The girl'?
questions.hint: The girl is one female person.

questions.prompt: THE CAR is red. Which pronoun replaces 'The car'?
questions.hint: A car is a thing, not a person.

sections.name: Subject-Verb Agreement
sections.instruction: The verb must match the subject - singular subjects need singular verbs.

questions.prompt: The dog ___ in the yard.
questions.hint: One dog (singular) needs 'runs' with an 's'.

questions.prompt: They ___ in Grade 2.
questions.hint: 'They' is plural, so we use 'are'.

questions.prompt: She ___ to read.
questions.hint: 'She' is one person, so the verb needs an 's'.

questions.prompt: The children ___ happy.
questions.hint: 'Children' means more than one child.

questions.prompt: My father ___ to work every day.
questions.hint: 'My father' is one person.

sections.name: Punctuation
sections.instruction: Use capital letters, full stops, and question marks correctly.

questions.prompt:
How should this sentence be written correctly?
i live in jamaica
questions.hint: Start with a capital letter. 'Jamaica' is a country name (capital J). End with a full stop.

questions.prompt:
How should this be written?
mrs grant is my teacher
questions.hint: 'Mrs' and 'Grant' are names - they need capital letters!

questions.prompt:
What punctuation goes at the end?
Where is your book
questions.hint: This is asking a question - 'Where'?

questions.prompt:
What punctuation goes at the end?
I am going to school
questions.hint: This is a statement, not a question.
